title: silene italica subsp. longicilia
intro: Silene italica subsp. longicilia, also known as Italian catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Silene italica subsp. longicilia include its ability to alleviate symptoms of asthma, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as tea or used topically to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
